title: cannot save image with empty image.ref.name
body:
**Description**
When attempting to use `buildah push` to `oci` or `oci-archive` an error message is displayed regarding empty `image.ref.name`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Follow documented method to buildah push to oci (https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/docs/buildah-push.md)

```
# buildah push 74a4ffb80cef oci:/tmp/imgdump
error pushing image "74a4ffb80cef" to "oci:/tmp/imgdump": error copying layers and metadata: Error initializing destination oci:/tmp/imgdump:: cannot save image with empty image.ref.name
```

2.
```
# buildah push 74a4ffb80cef oci-archive:/tmp/74a4ffb80cef
error pushing image "74a4ffb80cef" to "oci-archive:/tmp/74a4ffb80cef": error copying layers and metadata: Error initializing destination oci-archive:/tmp/74a4ffb80cef:: cannot save image with empty image.ref.name
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Error regarding empty `image.ref.name`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Export of OCI formatted image to provided directory.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-0.16-1.git82529cd.el7.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 0.16 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

**Output of `rpm -q runc`:**

```
runc-1.0.0-26.rc4.dev.git9f9c962.el7.x86_64
```

comments.body:
I set the following annotation in an image build using buildah:
`org.opencontainers.image.ref.name`

From the OCI spec here:
https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/master/annotations.md

But it didn't resolve the issue. Is there a change in something that buildah calls for the export?

Under `Destination` on the man page (https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/docs/buildah-push.md) it is stated that to push to oci the syntax must be `oci:path:tag`. The example later in the man page is misleading as it doe not have the `:tag`. Will open a PR to fix the documentation.

I think there is more that just a documentation fix here, the syntax could be checked before passing off the execution to provide a better response to the user.

eg.
`# buildah push 74a4ffb80cef oci:/tmp/imgdump`
`Error: the oci transport must use the following syntax oci:/path/to/layout:image:tag`

`# buildah push 74a4ffb80cef oci-archive:/tmp/74a4ffb80cef`
`Error: the oci-archive transport must use the following syntax oci-archive:/path/to/archive:image:tag`

Currently, the error doesn't clearly direct the user to a resolution.

The error returned mentions that is it missing an image reference name, which is the `image:tag` part. We support many different transports and the allowed syntax for each transport is slightly different and returning a specific error for each transport is not really worth it in my opinion. The man page is there precisely for this information. @rhatdan WDYT?

My concern is `image.ref.name` doesn't mean anything to the user in this context. The other argument is that the number of transports in buildah is fairly limited, and it would amount to essentially regex validation of the input in buildah before passing to the transport? Could the regex be defined as part of each of the transport definition/plugins in buildah?
The error message for oci and oci-archive is being reworded here https://github.com/containers/image/pull/439. docker-archive and dir already return a more detailed error message.
